full_response:
We work in Schwarzschild coordinates \((t,r,\theta,\phi)\) with metric (in units \(c=1\), \(G=1\))
  ds² = −(1−2M/r)\,dt² + (1−2M/r)^{−1}dr² + r²(dθ² + sin²θ dϕ²)  for  r>2M.

‣ 1.  Parametrization of the curve  
   We are given the world‐line  
     r(λ)=r₀ = const,  
     θ(λ)=θ₀ = const,  
     ϕ(λ)=ϕ₀ = const,  
   with only t varying.  Thus we may write  
     X^μ(λ) = ( t(λ), r₀, θ₀, ϕ₀ ) .

‣ 2.  Tangent 4‐vector  
   By definition the coordinate components of the tangent are  
     T^μ = dX^μ/dλ = ( dt/dλ, 0, 0, 0 ) .  
   Equivalently, \(T = (dt/dλ)\,\partial_t\).

‣ 3.  Choice of affine parameter  
   A natural choice is proper time \(\tau\) along the world‐line, so that  
     g_{μν}T^μT^ν = g_{tt}(dt/dτ)² = −1  
   ⇒  −(1−2M/r₀)(dt/dτ)² = −1  
   ⇒  (dt/dτ) = (1−2M/r₀)^{−1/2} .

‣ 4.  Unit‐normalized 4‐velocity  
   Thus in the coordinate basis \(\{\partial_t,\partial_r,\partial_θ,\partial_ϕ\}\) the unit tangent (4‐velocity) is  
     U^μ = ( U^t, U^r, U^θ, U^ϕ )  
          = ( (1−2M/r₀)^{−1/2}, 0, 0, 0 ) .

Boxing the final result:  

\boxed{ 
\text{Along }r=r₀,\;\theta=\theta₀,\;\phi=\phi₀,\;
\text{the unit‐normalized tangent (4‐velocity) is}
\quad
U^μ = \bigl(U^t,U^r,U^θ,U^ϕ\bigr)
= \bigl((1-2M/r₀)^{-1/2},\,0,\,0,\,0\bigr)\,. 
}  

Confidence: 100%
